title: Natural Health Products Ingredients Database Request Form - Canada.ca
content:
## Natural Health Products Ingredients Database Request Form
Natural Health Product Licensing Bulletin No. 4, July 11, 2025
- Ingredients and associated information must be present in the Natural Health Products Ingredients Database (NHPID) to be selected in the Natural Health Product Licence Application Form (web PLA form) or the Natural Health Product Licence Amendment and Notification Form (ANF) prior to submitting an application in accordance with the Natural Health Products Management of Applications Policy (NHP MAP).
- The Natural and Non-prescription Health Products Directorate (NNHPD) has developed a new form to request modifications to the NHPID (pdf).
- The new NHPID Request Form replaces the previous NHPID Issue Form, which is no longer operational.
- A new version of the NHP MAP will be published in Fall 2025 to reflect the updated process.
NNHPD is committed to continuously enhancing its service delivery, including streamlining the process for applicants to request modifications to the NHPID.
The new NHPID Request Form is used to request the addition, modification or deletion of ingredients and/or associated information in the NHPID. As part of the NHP Online System, it is not intended for use with other product lines.
In accordance with the NHP MAP , ingredients and associated information must be present in the NHPID prior to submitting an NHP application. Otherwise, the ingredient(s) and/or associated information cannot be selected in the Natural Health Product Licence Application Form (web PLA form) or the ANF, and applicants will not be able to complete their application.
The new NHPID Request Form replaces the NHPID Issue Form (.pdf) which was only available when requested directly from NNHPD. The new form guides users step by step through the process. Once completed, it will be automatically sent to NNHPD via the submit button.
NNHPD’s process to review completed forms will remain the same.  Clarification and/or additional information may be requested by NNHPD for the ingredient(s) and/or associated information to appear correctly in the NHPID in accordance with the Natural Health Products Online System Standard Terminology Guide . Requestors will  only be contacted if the information submitted requires clarification and/or is insufficient to proceed with the proposed NHPID modification(s).
NNHPD updates the NHPID on a monthly basis, typically on the last Friday of each month. Modifications made to the NHPID, since its last update, are listed on What’s New in the NHPID webpage following each load cycle of the NHPID.
For inquiries about this bulletin, please contact us by email: nnhpd-dpsnso@hc-sc.gc.ca .
